title: Word Search
questionBody:
Given an m x n grid of characters board and a string word, return true if word exists in the grid.

The word can be constructed from letters of sequentially adjacent cells, where adjacent cells are horizontally or vertically neighboring. The same letter cell may not be used more than once.

 

Example 1:

Input: board = [["A","B","C","E"],["S","F","C","S"],["A","D","E","E"]], word = "ABCCED"
Output: true

Example 2:

Input: board = [["A","B","C","E"],["S","F","C","S"],["A","D","E","E"]], word = "SEE"
Output: true

Example 3:

Input: board = [["A","B","C","E"],["S","F","C","S"],["A","D","E","E"]], word = "ABCB"
Output: false

 

Constraints:

  • m == board.length
  • n = board[i].length
  • 1 <= m, n <= 6
  • 1 <= word.length <= 15
  • board and word consists of only lowercase and uppercase English letters.

 

Follow up: Could you use search pruning to make your solution faster with a larger board?
plainEnglishBody:
Given a 2D board of characters (board) and a string word, determine if the word exists in the board.

The word can be constructed from letters of sequentially adjacent cells, where adjacent cells are horizontally or vertically neighboring.

The same letter cell may not be used more than once in a single word search.

Return true if the word exists in the board, and false otherwise.

examples.explanation: The word 'ABCCED' can be found starting from board[0][0], going right, down, right, down, right.
The word 'SEE' can be found starting from board[1][0], going right, down.
The word 'ABCB' cannot be found because the path would require revisiting a 'B' that's already been used.
The word 'A' can be found as the only element in the board.
The word 'ACDB' can be found by starting at board[0][0], going down, right, and down.

clarifyingQuestions: What are the dimensions of the board, and what is the maximum length of the word? This will help me understand the scale of the input.
Can the board contain characters other than letters? Are the letters in the word guaranteed to be present in the board?
Is the word case-sensitive? Should I treat 'A' and 'a' as the same letter or as different letters?
If the word can be found multiple times in the board, should I return true after finding the first instance, or do I need to find all possible instances?
Is the board guaranteed to be rectangular (i.e., all rows have the same number of columns)? What should I return if either the board or the word is empty or null?

edgeCases.handling: Return false immediately as no search is possible.
Return true immediately as an empty word is always found.
Return false immediately, word cannot be found.

edgeCases.edgeCase: Word longer than the total letters on the board
edgeCases.handling: Return false since the word cannot possibly exist on the board.

edgeCases.edgeCase: Word contains characters not present in the board
edgeCases.handling: The DFS search will not find a starting point and thus will return false.

edgeCases.edgeCase: Board and word contain only one character.
edgeCases.handling: The initial check and DFS will compare the characters and return true or false.

edgeCases.edgeCase: Word appears multiple times in the board (overlapping paths not allowed).
edgeCases.handling: The used character check prevents revisiting cells within a single search, ensuring each character in the board is used at most once per search attempt.

edgeCases.edgeCase: Large board and long word causing potential stack overflow in recursive DFS.
edgeCases.handling: The solution should consider iterative DFS with an explicit stack to handle extremely large inputs without stack overflow.

bruteForce.overview: Imagine searching for a word hidden in a grid of letters. The brute force method involves checking every possible path through the grid to see if it spells out the word. We'll explore all potential starting points and directions until we find the word or exhaust all possibilities.
steps: Begin by picking any letter in the grid as a potential starting point for the word.
From that starting letter, check all the letters next to it (up, down, left, right) to see if any of them match the second letter of the word we are searching for.

If we find a matching second letter, we then check all the letters next to that second letter (again, up, down, left, right) to see if they match the third letter of our word. It's important to remember we can't revisit the same letter we already used on this particular path.
Continue this process, extending the path, until we either find the entire word or reach a dead end (no matching letters are found next to the current letter).

If we reach a dead end, we backtrack and try a different path. If we can't find the word using this starting letter, we then select a different letter in the grid as the starting point and repeat the entire process.
We continue doing this for every single letter in the grid, exploring all possible paths until the word is found or all paths are exhausted.

bigO.runtimeExplanation: The algorithm iterates through each cell in the M x N board as a potential starting point. From each starting point, it explores all possible paths of length L (the length of the word), where at each step it has up to 4 possible directions to explore (up, down, left, right). Therefore, in the worst-case scenario, the time complexity is proportional to M * N (the number of starting points) multiplied by 4 raised to the power of L (the number of possible paths from each starting point). This gives a time complexity of O(M * N * 4^L).
bigO.spaceExplanation: The primary auxiliary space usage comes from the recursion depth of the depth-first search. In the worst-case scenario, the algorithm might explore almost all cells in the board before finding the word or determining it doesn't exist. The maximum recursion depth is bounded by the number of cells in the board, represented as M*N, where M is the number of rows and N is the number of columns in the board. Each recursive call adds a frame to the call stack. Therefore, the space complexity is O(M*N) due to the potential depth of the recursion.

Python.code:
def word_search(board, word):
    number_of_rows = len(board)
    number_of_columns = len(board[0])

    def depth_first_search(row, column, word_index, visited):
        if word_index == len(word):
            return True

        if (row < 0 or row >= number_of_rows or
            column < 0 or column >= number_of_columns or
            board[row][column] != word[word_index] or
            (row, column) in visited):
            return False

        # Mark the current cell as visited to avoid revisiting.
        visited.add((row, column))

        found = (depth_first_search(row + 1, column, word_index + 1, visited.copy()) or
                 depth_first_search(row - 1, column, word_index + 1, visited.copy()) or
                 depth_first_search(row, column + 1, word_index + 1, visited.copy()) or
                 depth_first_search(row, column - 1, word_index + 1, visited.copy()))

        return found

    # Iterate through each cell in the board as a starting point.
    for row in range(number_of_rows):
        for column in range(number_of_columns):
            # Initiate DFS from the current cell if it matches
            # the first character of the target word
            if board[row][column] == word[0]:
                if depth_first_search(row, column, 0, set()):
                    return True
    # No path found for the specified word
    return False

def word_search(board, word):
    number_of_rows = len(board)
    number_of_columns = len(board[0])

    def depth_first_search(row, column, word_index, visited):
        if word_index == len(word):
            return True

        if (row < 0 or row >= number_of_rows or
            column < 0 or column >= number_of_columns or
            board[row][column] != word[word_index] or
            (row, column) in visited):
            return False

        # Mark the current cell as visited to avoid revisiting.
        visited.add((row, column))

        found = (depth_first_search(row + 1, column, word_index + 1, visited.copy()) or
                 depth_first_search(row - 1, column, word_index + 1, visited.copy()) or
                 depth_first_search(row, column + 1, word_index + 1, visited.copy()) or
                 depth_first_search(row, column - 1, word_index + 1, visited.copy()))

        return found

    # Iterate through each cell in the board as a starting point.
    for row in range(number_of_rows):
        for column in range(number_of_columns):
            # Initiate DFS from the current cell if it matches
            # the first character of the target word
            if board[row][column] == word[0]:
                if depth_first_search(row, column, 0, set()):
                    return True
    # No path found for the specified word
    return False

JavaScript.html:
function wordSearch(board, word) {
  const rows = board.length;
  const cols = board[0].length;

  function dfs(rowPosition, colPosition, wordIndex, visited) {
    if (wordIndex === word.length) {
      return true;
    }

    if (
      rowPosition < 0 ||
      rowPosition >= rows ||
      colPosition < 0 ||
      colPosition >= cols ||
      board[rowPosition][colPosition] !== word[wordIndex] ||
      visited[rowPosition][colPosition]
    ) {
      return false;
    }

    visited[rowPosition][colPosition] = true;

    // Explore all 4 directions
    const found = 
      dfs(rowPosition + 1, colPosition, wordIndex + 1, visited) ||
      dfs(rowPosition - 1, colPosition, wordIndex + 1, visited) ||
      dfs(rowPosition, colPosition + 1, wordIndex + 1, visited) ||
      dfs(rowPosition, colPosition - 1, wordIndex + 1, visited);

    visited[rowPosition][colPosition] = false; // Backtrack
    return found;
  }

  for (let rowPosition = 0; rowPosition < rows; rowPosition++) {
    for (let colPosition = 0; colPosition < cols; colPosition++) {
      // Initiate search from every cell
      if (board[rowPosition][colPosition] === word[0]) {
        const visited = Array(rows).fill(null).map(() => Array(cols).fill(false));

        // Check if the word can be formed from this starting point
        if (dfs(rowPosition, colPosition, 0, visited)) {
          return true;
        }
      }
    }
  }

  return false;
}

TypeScript.html:
function wordSearchBruteForce(board: string[][], word: string): boolean {
  const numberOfRows: number = board.length;
  const numberOfColumns: number = board[0].length;

  // Iterate through each cell in the board as a potential starting point.
  for (let rowIndex: number = 0; rowIndex < numberOfRows; rowIndex++) {
    for (let columnIndex: number = 0; columnIndex < numberOfColumns; columnIndex++) {
      if (explorePath(board, word, rowIndex, columnIndex, 0, new Set<string>())) {
        return true;
      }
    }
  }

  return false;

  function explorePath(
    board: string[][],
    word: string,
    rowIndex: number,
    columnIndex: number,
    wordIndex: number,
    visited: Set<string>
  ): boolean {
    // If the current index equals word length, word is found.
    if (wordIndex === word.length) {
      return true;
    }

    // Check for out-of-bounds or letter mismatch.
    if (
      rowIndex < 0 ||
      rowIndex >= numberOfRows ||
      columnIndex < 0 ||
      columnIndex >= numberOfColumns ||
      board[rowIndex][columnIndex] !== word[wordIndex]
    ) {
      return false;
    }

    const cellKey: string = `${rowIndex}-${columnIndex}`
    if (visited.has(cellKey)) {
        return false;
    }

    // Mark the current cell as visited to avoid cycles
    visited.add(cellKey);

    // Explore adjacent cells recursively
    const found: boolean =
      explorePath(board, word, rowIndex + 1, columnIndex, wordIndex + 1, new Set(visited)) ||
      explorePath(board, word, rowIndex - 1, columnIndex, wordIndex + 1, new Set(visited)) ||
      explorePath(board, word, rowIndex, columnIndex + 1, wordIndex + 1, new Set(visited)) ||
      explorePath(board, word, rowIndex, columnIndex - 1, wordIndex + 1, new Set(visited));

    if (found) {
      return true;
    } else {
      return false;
    }
  }
}

Java.html:
class Solution {
    public boolean exist(char[][] board, String word) {
        int rows = board.length;
        int cols = board[0].length;

        for (int row = 0; row < rows; row++) {
            for (int col = 0; col < cols; col++) {
                // Start the search from every cell
                if (board[row][col] == word.charAt(0) &&
                    exploreBoard(board, row, col, word, 0)) {
                    return true;
                }
            }
        }
        return false;
    }

    private boolean exploreBoard(char[][] board, int row, int col, String word, int wordIndex) {
        // Word found, base case for recursion
        if (wordIndex == word.length()) {
            return true;
        }

        // Out of bounds or character mismatch
        if (row < 0 || row >= board.length || col < 0 || col >= board[0].length ||
            board[row][col] != word.charAt(wordIndex)) {
            return false;
        }

        char temp = board[row][col];
        board[row][col] = '*';

        // Explore all 4 directions
        boolean found = 
            exploreBoard(board, row + 1, col, word, wordIndex + 1) ||
            exploreBoard(board, row - 1, col, word, wordIndex + 1) ||
            exploreBoard(board, row, col + 1, word, wordIndex + 1) ||
            exploreBoard(board, row, col - 1, word, wordIndex + 1);

        // Backtrack to reset the board's original value 
        board[row][col] = temp;

        return found;
    }
}

C++.html:
#include <vector>
#include <string>

using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    bool exist(vector<vector<char>>& board, string word) {
        int rows = board.size();
        int columns = board[0].size();

        for (int row = 0; row < rows; ++row) {
            for (int column = 0; column < columns; ++column) {
                if (board[row][column] == word[0]) {
                    vector<vector<bool>> visited(rows, vector<bool>(columns, false));
                    if (findWord(board, word, row, column, 0, visited)) {
                        return true;
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return false;
    }

private:
    bool findWord(vector<vector<char>>& board, string word, int row, int column, int wordIndex, vector<vector<bool>>& visited) {
        if (wordIndex == word.length()) {
            return true;
        }

        int rows = board.size();
        int columns = board[0].size();

        if (row < 0 || row >= rows || column < 0 || column >= columns || visited[row][column] || board[row][column] != word[wordIndex]) {
            return false;
        }

        visited[row][column] = true;

        // Explore all 4 directions
        // Need to explore all paths from this point
        if (
            findWord(board, word, row + 1, column, wordIndex + 1, visited) ||
            findWord(board, word, row - 1, column, wordIndex + 1, visited) ||
            findWord(board, word, row, column + 1, wordIndex + 1, visited) ||
            findWord(board, word, row, column - 1, wordIndex + 1, visited)
        ) {
            return true;
        }

        // Backtrack: Reset the visited status to explore other paths.
        visited[row][column] = false;
        return false;
    }
};

C#.html:
public class Solution {
    public bool Exist(char[][] board, string word) {
        int rows = board.Length;
        int cols = board[0].Length;

        for (int row = 0; row < rows; row++)
        {
            for (int col = 0; col < cols; col++)
            {
                // Start searching from every cell
                if (ExistHelper(board, row, col, word, 0))
                {
                    return true;
                }
            }
        }

        return false;
    }

    private bool ExistHelper(char[][] board, int row, int col, string word, int wordIndex)
    {
        // Base case: word found
        if (wordIndex == word.Length)
        {
            return true;
        }

        // Check boundaries and if the character matches
        if (row < 0 || row >= board.Length || col < 0 || col >= board[0].Length || board[row][col] != word[wordIndex])
        {
            return false;
        }

        char temp = board[row][col];

        // Mark the current cell as visited to avoid cycles
        board[row][col] = '*';

        // Explore all four directions
        bool found = ExistHelper(board, row + 1, col, word, wordIndex + 1) ||
                     ExistHelper(board, row - 1, col, word, wordIndex + 1) ||
                     ExistHelper(board, row, col + 1, word, wordIndex + 1) ||
                     ExistHelper(board, row, col - 1, word, wordIndex + 1);

        // Backtrack: restore the original character
        board[row][col] = temp;

        return found;
    }
}

Go.html:
package main

func wordSearch(board [][]byte, word string) bool {
	rows := len(board)
	cols := len(board[0])

	var backtrack func(row int, col int, wordIndex int) bool
	backtrack = func(row int, col int, wordIndex int) bool {
		if wordIndex == len(word) {
			return true
		}

		if row < 0 || row >= rows || col < 0 || col >= cols || board[row][col] != word[wordIndex] {
			return false
		}

		originalCharacter := board[row][col]
		// Mark the current cell as visited to avoid cycles.
		board[row][col] = '#'

		// Explore all four possible directions.
		found := backtrack(row+1, col, wordIndex+1) || \
			backtrack(row-1, col, wordIndex+1) || \
			backtrack(row, col+1, wordIndex+1) || \
			backtrack(row, col-1, wordIndex+1)

		// Backtrack: restore the original character.
		board[row][col] = originalCharacter

		return found
	}

	// Iterate through each cell in the board.
	for row := 0; row < rows; row++ {
		for col := 0; col < cols; col++ {
			// Start the search from each cell.
			if backtrack(row, col, 0) {
				return true
			}
		}
	}

	return false
}

Rust.html:
pub fn word_search(board: Vec<Vec<char>>, word: String) -> bool {
    let rows = board.len();
    let cols = if rows > 0 { board[0].len() } else { 0 };
    let word_chars: Vec<char> = word.chars().collect();

    for row_index in 0..rows {
        for col_index in 0..cols {
            // Start searching from every cell in the board
            if search_from_cell(&board, &word_chars, row_index, col_index, 0) {
                return true;
            }
        }
    }

    false
}

fn search_from_cell(
    board: &Vec<Vec<char>>,
    word_chars: &Vec<char>,
    row_index: usize,
    col_index: usize,
    word_index: usize,
) -> bool {
    if word_index == word_chars.len() {
        return true;
    }

    if row_index >= board.len()
        || col_index >= board[0].len()
        || board[row_index][col_index] != word_chars[word_index]
    {
        return false;
    }

    let mut current_board = board.clone();
    let current_char = current_board[row_index][col_index];

    // Mark the current cell as visited to avoid cycles
    current_board[row_index][col_index] = '#';

    // Explore all 4 possible directions
    let found = search_from_cell(
        &current_board,
        word_chars,
        row_index + 1,
        col_index,
        word_index + 1,
    ) || search_from_cell(
        &current_board,
        word_chars,
        row_index.wrapping_sub(1),
        col_index,
        word_index + 1,
    ) || search_from_cell(
        &current_board,
        word_chars,
        row_index,
        col_index + 1,
        word_index + 1,
    ) || search_from_cell(
        &current_board,
        word_chars,
        row_index,
        col_index.wrapping_sub(1),
        word_index + 1,
    );

    current_board[row_index][col_index] = current_char;

    found
}

Kotlin.html:
fun wordSearchBruteForce(board: Array<CharArray>, word: String): Boolean {
    val numberOfRows = board.size
    val numberOfColumns = board[0].size

    for (rowIndex in 0 until numberOfRows) {
        for (columnIndex in 0 until numberOfColumns) {
            // Start searching from each cell in the board.
            if (explorePath(board, word, rowIndex, columnIndex, 0, Array(numberOfRows) { BooleanArray(numberOfColumns) })) {
                return true
            }
        }
    }

    return false
}

fun explorePath(board: Array<CharArray>, word: String, rowIndex: Int, columnIndex: Int, wordIndex: Int, visited: Array<BooleanArray>): Boolean {
    // If the word is found, return true
    if (wordIndex == word.length) {
        return true
    }

    if (rowIndex < 0 || rowIndex >= board.size || columnIndex < 0 || columnIndex >= board[0].size || visited[rowIndex][columnIndex] || board[rowIndex][columnIndex] != word[wordIndex]) {
        return false
    }

    // Mark the current cell as visited to avoid cycles
    visited[rowIndex][columnIndex] = true

    // Explore all four possible directions
    if (explorePath(board, word, rowIndex + 1, columnIndex, wordIndex + 1, visited)) {
        return true
    }
    
    if (explorePath(board, word, rowIndex - 1, columnIndex, wordIndex + 1, visited)) {
        return true
    }

    if (explorePath(board, word, rowIndex, columnIndex + 1, wordIndex + 1, visited)) {
        return true
    }

    if (explorePath(board, word, rowIndex, columnIndex - 1, wordIndex + 1, visited)) {
        return true
    }

    // Backtrack: unmark the current cell as visited
    visited[rowIndex][columnIndex] = false

    return false
}

Swift.html:
func wordSearchBruteForce(board: [[Character]], word: String) -> Bool {
    let numberOfRows = board.count
    let numberOfColumns = board[0].count
    let wordLength = word.count
    let wordCharacters = Array(word)

    func depthFirstSearch(rowIndex: Int, columnIndex: Int, wordIndex: Int, visited: inout [[Bool]]) -> Bool {
        // If we found the entire word, return true.
        if wordIndex == wordLength {
            return true
        }

        // Check for out-of-bounds or visited cells or mismatch.
        if rowIndex < 0 || rowIndex >= numberOfRows || columnIndex < 0 || columnIndex >= numberOfColumns || visited[rowIndex][columnIndex] || board[rowIndex][columnIndex] != wordCharacters[wordIndex] {
            return false
        }

        // Mark the current cell as visited to avoid cycles.
        visited[rowIndex][columnIndex] = true

        if depthFirstSearch(rowIndex: rowIndex + 1, columnIndex: columnIndex, wordIndex: wordIndex + 1, visited: &visited) {
            return true
        }
        if depthFirstSearch(rowIndex: rowIndex - 1, columnIndex: columnIndex, wordIndex: wordIndex + 1, visited: &visited) {
            return true
        }
        if depthFirstSearch(rowIndex: rowIndex, columnIndex: columnIndex + 1, wordIndex: wordIndex + 1, visited: &visited) {
            return true
        }
        if depthFirstSearch(rowIndex: rowIndex, columnIndex: columnIndex - 1, wordIndex: wordIndex + 1, visited: &visited) {
            return true
        }

        // Backtrack: unmark the current cell to explore other paths.
        visited[rowIndex][columnIndex] = false
        return false
    }

    // Iterate through each cell to start the search.
    for rowIndex in 0..<numberOfRows {
        for columnIndex in 0..<numberOfColumns {
            // Use a visited matrix to prevent re-visiting cells on same path
            var visited = Array(repeating: Array(repeating: false, count: numberOfColumns), count: numberOfRows)

            // Start DFS from each cell.
            if depthFirstSearch(rowIndex: rowIndex, columnIndex: columnIndex, wordIndex: 0, visited: &visited) {
                return true
            }
        }
    }

    // If no path found, return false.
    return false
}

Ruby.code:
def word_search_brute_force(board, word)
  rows = board.length
  cols = board[0].length

  (0...rows).each do |row_index|
    (0...cols).each do |col_index|
      # Check every cell for the starting letter
      if board[row_index][col_index] == word[0]
        if backtrack(board, row_index, col_index, word, 0)
          return true
        end
      end
    end
  end
  return false
end

def backtrack(board, row_index, col_index, word, word_index)
  # Base case: Word completely found
  if word_index == word.length
    return true
  end

  # Check boundaries and characters matching
  if row_index < 0 || row_index >= board.length || col_index < 0 || col_index >= board[0].length || board[row_index][col_index] != word[word_index]
    return false
  end

  current_character = board[row_index][col_index]
  board[row_index][col_index] = '#'

  # Explore all possible directions
  directions = [[0, 1], [0, -1], [1, 0], [-1, 0]]
  directions.each do |row_offset, col_offset|
    new_row = row_index + row_offset
    new_col = col_index + col_offset
    # Try each direction recursively
    if backtrack(board, new_row, new_col, word, word_index + 1)
      return true
    end
  end
  # Reset current character after all directions have been checked
  board[row_index][col_index] = current_character
  return false
end

  rows = board.length
  cols = board[0].length

  (0...rows).each do |row_index|
    (0...cols).each do |col_index|
      # Check every cell for the starting letter
      if board[row_index][col_index] == word[0]
        if backtrack(board, row_index, col_index, word, 0)
          return true
        end
      end
    end
  end
  return false
end

def backtrack(board, row_index, col_index, word, word_index)
  # Base case: Word completely found
  if word_index == word.length
    return true
  end

  # Check boundaries and characters matching
  if row_index < 0 || row_index >= board.length || col_index < 0 || col_index >= board[0].length || board[row_index][col_index] != word[word_index]
    return false
  end

  current_character = board[row_index][col_index]
  board[row_index][col_index] = '#'

  # Explore all possible directions
  directions = [[0, 1], [0, -1], [1, 0], [-1, 0]]
  directions.each do |row_offset, col_offset|
    new_row = row_index + row_offset
    new_col = col_index + col_offset
    # Try each direction recursively
    if backtrack(board, new_row, new_col, word, word_index + 1)
      return true
    end
  end
  # Reset current character after all directions have been checked
  board[row_index][col_index] = current_character
  return false
end

PHP.code:
<?php
function wordSearchBruteForce(array $board, string $word): bool
{
    $boardHeight = count($board);
    $boardWidth = count($board[0]);

    for ($row = 0; $row < $boardHeight; $row++) {
        for ($col = 0; $col < $boardWidth; $col++) {
            // Check if the current cell is the start of the word.
            if ($board[$row][$col] === $word[0]) {
                if (exploreWord($board, $word, $row, $col, 0, array())) {
                    return true;
                }
            }
        }
    }

    return false;
}

function exploreWord(array $board, string $word, int $row, int $col, int $wordIndex, array $visited):
bool
{
    // Base case: Word found
    if ($wordIndex === strlen($word)) {
        return true;
    }

    // Out-of-bounds check or already visited cell.
    if ($row < 0 || $row >= count($board) || $col < 0 || $col >= count($board[0])) {
        return false;
    }

    $currentCellKey = $row . '-' . $col;
    if (in_array($currentCellKey, $visited)) {
        return false;
    }

    // Check if current board character matches the word.
    if ($board[$row][$col] !== $word[$wordIndex]) {
        return false;
    }

    $visited[] = $currentCellKey;

    // Explore all four directions.
    if (
        exploreWord($board, $word, $row + 1, $col, $wordIndex + 1, $visited) ||
        exploreWord($board, $word, $row - 1, $col, $wordIndex + 1, $visited) ||
        exploreWord($board, $word, $row, $col + 1, $wordIndex + 1, $visited) ||
        exploreWord($board, $word, $row, $col - 1, $wordIndex + 1, $visited)
    ) {
        return true;
    }

    // Backtrack: Remove the current cell from the path.
    array_pop($visited);
    return false;
}

?>

optimal.overview: This problem asks us to find a word in a grid of letters. The optimal strategy involves systematically exploring possible paths in the grid, checking if the letters along the path match the letters of the word we're searching for. We avoid re-exploring paths we've already tried.
steps: Start by looking at each letter in the grid as a potential starting point for the word.
If a letter matches the first letter of the word, explore the neighboring letters (up, down, left, and right) to see if they match the second letter of the word.
Continue exploring adjacent letters, building the path one letter at a time, making sure you only move to letters that match the next letter in the word.
To avoid getting stuck in cycles, keep track of which letters in the grid you've already visited along a particular path, and don't revisit them.
If you find a path that spells out the entire word, you've found a match and can stop searching.
If you explore all possible paths from all starting points and don't find the word, it doesn't exist in the grid.

bigO.runtimeExplanation: Let M be the number of rows and N be the number of columns in the board, and L be the length of the word. The algorithm iterates through each cell in the M x N board, giving us M * N starting points. From each starting point, we explore at most 4 possible directions (up, down, left, right) for each letter in the word. In the worst-case scenario, we might explore almost every possible path of length L from each starting cell before either finding the word or exhausting all possibilities. This leads to a branching factor of 4 for each of the L letters in the word, hence 4^L. Therefore, the total time complexity is approximately M * N * 4^L.
bigO.spaceExplanation: The primary auxiliary space usage comes from the 'visited' set used to keep track of visited cells during the depth-first search, where M is the number of rows and N is the number of columns in the board. In the worst-case scenario, the recursive calls might explore almost all cells on the board, leading to a 'visited' set containing close to all board positions. Therefore the space complexity of the visited set would be O(M*N), since in worst case, we may visit all cells. The recursion depth will also be limited by the size of the board, contributing O(M*N) in the worst case, but this is accounted for in the visited set already.

Python.html:
def word_search(board, word):
    number_of_rows = len(board)
    number_of_columns = len(board[0])

    def depth_first_search(row, column, word_index, visited_cells):
        if word_index == len(word):
            return True

        if row < 0 or row >= number_of_rows or column < 0 or column >= number_of_columns or (row, column) in visited_cells or board[row][column] != word[word_index]:
            return False

        # Mark current cell as visited to prevent cycles
        visited_cells.add((row, column))

        found = (depth_first_search(row + 1, column, word_index + 1, visited_cells.copy()) or
                 depth_first_search(row - 1, column, word_index + 1, visited_cells.copy()) or
                 depth_first_search(row, column + 1, word_index + 1, visited_cells.copy()) or
                 depth_first_search(row, column - 1, word_index + 1, visited_cells.copy()))

        return found

    # Iterate through each cell on the board
    for row in range(number_of_rows):
        for column in range(number_of_columns):
            # Initiate DFS search from the matching cell
            if board[row][column] == word[0]:
                if depth_first_search(row, column, 0, set()):
                    return True

    # If word not found, return false
    return False

JavaScript.html:
function wordSearch(board, word) {
  const numberOfRows = board.length;
  const numberOfColumns = board[0].length;

  function depthFirstSearch(row, column, wordIndex, visited) {
    // If the entire word is found, stop.
    if (wordIndex === word.length) {
      return true;
    }

    //Boundary checks and matching letter check.
    if (
      row < 0 ||
      row >= numberOfRows ||
      column < 0 ||
      column >= numberOfColumns ||
      board[row][column] !== word[wordIndex] ||
      visited[row][column]
    ) {
      return false;
    }

    visited[row][column] = true;

    // Explore all 4 directions.
    const found = 
      depthFirstSearch(row + 1, column, wordIndex + 1, visited) ||
      depthFirstSearch(row - 1, column, wordIndex + 1, visited) ||
      depthFirstSearch(row, column + 1, wordIndex + 1, visited) ||
      depthFirstSearch(row, column - 1, wordIndex + 1, visited);

    visited[row][column] = false; // Backtrack: clear the path
    return found;
  }

  //Iterate through the board to find the starting letter
  for (let row = 0; row < numberOfRows; row++) {
    for (let column = 0; column < numberOfColumns; column++) {
      //Create a visited matrix for each starting point
      const visited = Array(numberOfRows).fill(null).map(() => Array(numberOfColumns).fill(false));

      // Begin DFS if first letter matches
      if (board[row][column] === word[0]) {
        // Start depth first search from each cell
        if (depthFirstSearch(row, column, 0, visited)) {
          return true;
        }
      }
    }
  }

  return false;
}

TypeScript.html:
function wordSearch(board: string[][], word: string): boolean {
  const numberOfRows: number = board.length;
  const numberOfColumns: number = board[0].length;

  // Iterate through each cell as a potential starting point
  for (let row = 0; row < numberOfRows; row++) {
    for (let column = 0; column < numberOfColumns; column++) {
      if (board[row][column] === word[0]) {
        //If the first letter matches, start DFS
        if (depthFirstSearch(board, row, column, word, 0, new Set<string>())) {
          return true;
        }
      }
    }
  }

  return false;

  function depthFirstSearch(
    board: string[][],
    row: number,
    column: number,
    word: string,
    index: number,
    visited: Set<string>
  ): boolean {
    // If the current index equals the length of the word, we found the word
    if (index === word.length) {
      return true;
    }

    // Boundary checks and visited check
    if (
      row < 0 ||
      row >= numberOfRows ||
      column < 0 ||
      column >= numberOfColumns ||
      board[row][column] !== word[index] ||
      visited.has(`${row}-${column}`)
    ) {
      return false;
    }

    // Mark the current cell as visited
    visited.add(`${row}-${column}`);

    // Explore adjacent cells
    const found =
      depthFirstSearch(board, row + 1, column, word, index + 1, new Set(visited))
      || depthFirstSearch(board, row - 1, column, word, index + 1, new Set(visited))
      || depthFirstSearch(board, row, column + 1, word, index + 1, new Set(visited))
      || depthFirstSearch(board, row, column - 1, word, index + 1, new Set(visited));

    return found;
  }
}

Java.html:
public class Solution {

    public boolean exist(char[][] board, String word) {
        int numberOfRows = board.length;
        int numberOfColumns = board[0].length;

        for (int row = 0; row < numberOfRows; row++) {
            for (int column = 0; column < numberOfColumns; column++) {
                // Start DFS from each cell
                if (board[row][column] == word.charAt(0) &&
                    depthFirstSearch(board, row, column, word, 0, new boolean[numberOfRows][numberOfColumns])) {
                    return true;
                }
            }
        }
        return false;
    }

    private boolean depthFirstSearch(char[][] board, int row, int column, String word,
                                       int index, boolean[][] visited) {
        // Base case: Word found
        if (index == word.length()) {
            return true;
        }

        int numberOfRows = board.length;
        int numberOfColumns = board[0].length;

        // Boundary check and visited check
        if (row < 0 || row >= numberOfRows || column < 0 || column >= numberOfColumns ||
            visited[row][column] || board[row][column] != word.charAt(index)) {
            return false;
        }

        // Mark current cell as visited
        visited[row][column] = true;

        // Explore adjacent cells
        // Search up
        if (depthFirstSearch(board, row - 1, column, word, index + 1, visited)) {
            return true;
        }

        // Search down
        if (depthFirstSearch(board, row + 1, column, word, index + 1, visited)) {
            return true;
        }

        // Search left
        if (depthFirstSearch(board, row, column - 1, word, index + 1, visited)) {
            return true;
        }

        // Search right
        if (depthFirstSearch(board, row, column + 1, word, index + 1, visited)) {
            return true;
        }

        // Backtrack: Unmark current cell
        // VERY IMPORTANT STEP
        // Backtracking is necessary to explore other paths.
        visited[row][column] = false;

        return false;
    }
}

C++.html:
#include <vector>
#include <string>

using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    bool exist(vector<vector<char>>& board, string word) {
        int number_of_rows = board.size();
        int number_of_columns = board[0].size();

        for (int row = 0; row < number_of_rows; ++row) {
            for (int col = 0; col < number_of_columns; ++col) {
                // Start DFS from each cell
                if (depthFirstSearch(board, row, col, word, 0)) {
                    return true;
                }
            }
        }
        return false;
    }

private:
    bool depthFirstSearch(vector<vector<char>>& board, int row, int col, string word, int index) {
        // Base case: word found
        if (index == word.length()) {
            return true;
        }

        // Check boundaries and if the current cell matches the character
        if (row < 0 || row >= board.size() || col < 0 || col >= board[0].size() || board[row][col] != word[index]) {
            return false;
        }

        // Mark the current cell as visited
        char temp = board[row][col];
        board[row][col] = '*';

        // Explore adjacent cells
        //We explore all 4 directions if our current position
is on the word's path
        bool found = depthFirstSearch(board, row + 1, col, word, index + 1) ||
                     depthFirstSearch(board, row - 1, col, word, index + 1) ||
                     depthFirstSearch(board, row, col + 1, word, index + 1) ||
                     depthFirstSearch(board, row, col - 1, word, index + 1);

        // Backtrack: restore the original value
        //If no path is found, we reset to previous state so that
        //later searches can use it.
        board[row][col] = temp;

        return found;
    }
};

C#.html:
public class Solution {
    public bool Exist(char[][] board, string word) {
        int rows = board.Length;
        int columns = board[0].Length;

        for (int row = 0; row < rows; row++)
        {
            for (int column = 0; column < columns; column++)
            {
                // Iterate through each cell for a possible start
                if (board[row][column] == word[0] && DepthFirstSearch(board, row, column, word, 0))
                {
                    return true;
                }
            }
        }

        return false;
    }

    private bool DepthFirstSearch(char[][] board, int row, int column, string word, int index) {
        // Check word length, done searching
        if (index == word.Length)
        {
            return true;
        }

        // Boundary check
        if (row < 0 || row >= board.Length || column < 0 || column >= board[0].Length || board[row][column] != word[index])
        {
            return false;
        }

        char temp = board[row][column];
        board[row][column] = '*';

        // Need to mark visited to prevent cycles.
        int[] rowOffset = { 0, 0, 1, -1 };
        int[] columnOffset = { 1, -1, 0, 0 };

        for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++)
        {
            int newRow = row + rowOffset[i];
            int newColumn = column + columnOffset[i];

            if (DepthFirstSearch(board, newRow, newColumn, word, index + 1))
            {
                return true;
            }
        }

        // Reset the value
        board[row][column] = temp; 
        return false;
    }
  }

Go.html:
package main

func exist(board [][]byte, word string) bool {
	rows := len(board)
	cols := len(board[0])

	for row := 0; row < rows; row++ {
		for col := 0; col < cols; col++ {
			if board[row][col] == word[0] {
				// Initiate DFS from each possible starting cell
				if dfs(board, row, col, word, 0, make([][]bool, rows)) {
					return true
				}
			}
		}
	}
	return false
}

func dfs(board [][]byte, row int, col int, word string, index int, visited [][]bool) bool {
	if index == len(word) {
		return true
	}

	if row < 0 || row >= len(board) || col < 0 || col >= len(board[0]) || board[row][col] != word[index] {
		return false
	}

	if visited[row] == nil {
		visited[row] = make([]bool, len(board[0]))
	}

	// Prevent cycles by marking visited cells
	if visited[row][col] {
		return false
	}

	visited[row][col] = true

	// Explore adjacent cells to find next character
	found := dfs(board, row+1, col, word, index+1, visited) ||
			dfs(board, row-1, col, word, index+1, visited) ||
			dfs(board, row, col+1, word, index+1, visited) ||
			dfs(board, row, col-1, word, index+1, visited)

	// Backtrack: mark current cell as unvisited
	visited[row][col] = false

	return found
}

Rust.html:
fn word_search(board: Vec<Vec<char>>, word: String) -> bool {
    let rows = board.len();
    let cols = board[0].len();
    let word_chars: Vec<char> = word.chars().collect();

    for row_index in 0..rows {
      for col_index in 0..cols {
        // Start DFS from each cell.
        if explore(row_index, col_index, &board, &word_chars, 0, &mut vec![vec![false; cols]; rows]) {
          return true;
        }
      }
    }

    false
  }

  fn explore(
    row_index: usize,
    col_index: usize,
    board: &Vec<Vec<char>>,
    word_chars: &Vec<char>,
    char_index: usize,
    visited: &mut Vec<Vec<bool>>,
  ) -> bool {
    //Base case: If the character index is the length of the word, we found it
    if char_index == word_chars.len() {
      return true;
    }

    let rows = board.len();
    let cols = board[0].len();

    if row_index >= rows || col_index >= cols || board[row_index][col_index] != word_chars[char_index] || visited[row_index][col_index]{
      return false;
    }

    visited[row_index][col_index] = true;

    // Explore adjacent cells
    let found = explore(row_index + 1, col_index, board, word_chars, char_index + 1, visited)
      || explore(row_index.wrapping_sub(1), col_index, board, word_chars, char_index + 1, visited)
      || explore(row_index, col_index + 1, board, word_chars, char_index + 1, visited)
      || explore(row_index, col_index.wrapping_sub(1), board, word_chars, char_index + 1, visited);

    // Backtrack. Important to reset the visited state
    visited[row_index][col_index] = false;

    found
  }
}

Kotlin.html:
fun exist(board: Array<CharArray>, word: String): Boolean {
    val numberOfRows = board.size
    val numberOfColumns = board[0].size

    for (row in 0 until numberOfRows) {
      for (column in 0 until numberOfColumns) {
        // Initiate DFS from each cell
        if (exploreBoard(board, row, column, word, 0, Array(numberOfRows) { BooleanArray(numberOfColumns) })) {
          return true
        }
      }
    }
    return false
  }

  fun exploreBoard(
    board: Array<CharArray>,
    row: Int,
    column: Int,
    word: String,
    wordIndex: Int,
    visited: Array<BooleanArray>
  ): Boolean {
    //Base case, word has been found
    if (wordIndex == word.length) {
      return true
    }

    //Boundary checks and character matching
    if (
      row < 0 ||
      row >= board.size ||
      column < 0 ||
      column >= board[0].size ||
      board[row][column] != word[wordIndex] ||
      visited[row][column]
    ) {
      return false
    }

    //Mark the current cell as visited
    visited[row][column] = true

    // Explore all 4 directions recursively.
    // If any direction finds the word, return true.
    // Otherwise, backtrack and unmark the cell.
    if (
      exploreBoard(board, row + 1, column, word, wordIndex + 1, visited) ||
      exploreBoard(board, row - 1, column, word, wordIndex + 1, visited) ||
      exploreBoard(board, row, column + 1, word, wordIndex + 1, visited) ||
      exploreBoard(board, row, column - 1, word, wordIndex + 1, visited)
    ) {
      return true
    }

    //Backtrack: unmark the cell
    visited[row][column] = false
    return false
  }
}

Swift.html:
struct CodeBlock {
	var code: String
}

func wordSearch(board: [[Character]], word: String) -> Bool {
	let numberOfRows = board.count
	let numberOfColumns = board[0].count
	let wordLength = word.count

	guard wordLength > 0 else { return true }

	for row in 0..<numberOfRows {
		for column in 0..<numberOfColumns {
			// Iterate through each cell as starting point.
			if wordSearchHelper(board: board, row: row, column: column, word: word, wordIndex: 0, visited: &[[Bool]](repeating: [Bool](repeating: false, count: numberOfColumns), count: numberOfRows)) {
				return true
			}
		}
	}

	return false
}

func wordSearchHelper(board: [[Character]], row: Int, column: Int, word: String, wordIndex: Int, visited: inout [[Bool]]) -> Bool {
	// Base case: If the current index equals to the length of word.
	if wordIndex == word.count {
		return true
	}

	let numberOfRows = board.count
	let numberOfColumns = board[0].count

	// Check if the current cell is valid.
	if row < 0 || row >= numberOfRows || column < 0 || column >= numberOfColumns || visited[row][column] {
		return false
	}

	let currentIndex = word.index(word.startIndex, offsetBy: wordIndex)

	// Check if the current letter matches.
	if board[row][column] != word[currentIndex] {
		return false
	}

	// Mark the current cell as visited to avoid cycles.
	visited[row][column] = true

	// Explore adjacent cells.
	let found = wordSearchHelper(board: board, row: row + 1, column: column, word: word, wordIndex: wordIndex + 1, visited: &visited) ||
				wordSearchHelper(board: board, row: row - 1, column: column, word: word, wordIndex: wordIndex + 1, visited: &visited) ||
				wordSearchHelper(board: board, row: row, column: column + 1, word: word, wordIndex: wordIndex + 1, visited: &visited) ||
				wordSearchHelper(board: board, row: row, column: column - 1, word: word, wordIndex: wordIndex + 1, visited: &visited)

	// Backtrack: Unmark the current cell.
	visited[row][column] = false

	return found
}

Ruby.code:
def word_search(board, word)
  number_of_rows = board.length
  number_of_columns = board[0].length

  def depth_first_search(row_index, column_index, word_index)
    # If we've found the entire word, we're done.
    return true if word_index == word.length

    # Check for out-of-bounds or mismatch.
    return false if row_index < 0 || row_index >= number_of_rows ||
                      column_index < 0 || column_index >= number_of_columns ||
                      board[row_index][column_index] != word[word_index]

    current_character = board[row_index][column_index]
    board[row_index][column_index] = '#'

    # Explore all 4 directions.
    found = depth_first_search(row_index + 1, column_index, word_index + 1) ||
            depth_first_search(row_index - 1, column_index, word_index + 1) ||
            depth_first_search(row_index, column_index + 1, word_index + 1) ||
            depth_first_search(row_index, column_index - 1, word_index + 1)

    # Backtrack: Restore the original character.
    board[row_index][column_index] = current_character
    return found
  end

  # Iterate through each cell of the board
  (0...number_of_rows).each do |row_index|
    (0...number_of_columns).each do |column_index|
      # Start DFS search from each cell
      if board[row_index][column_index] == word[0]

        # Initiate depth first search to find the word
        if depth_first_search(row_index, column_index, 0)
          return true
        end

      end
    end
  end

  # If no path results in finding the word.
  return false
end

    end
  end

  # If no path results in finding the word.
  return false
end

PHP.code:
<?php
function wordSearch(array $board, string $word): bool
{
    $rows = count($board);
    $cols = count($board[0]);

    for ($row = 0; $row < $rows; $row++) {
        for ($col = 0; $col < $cols; $col++) {
            // Start DFS from each cell.
            if (wordSearchRecursive($board, $word, $row, $col, 0)) {
                return true;
            }
        }
    }

    return false;
}

function wordSearchRecursive(array $board, string $word, int $row, int $col, int $wordIndex): bool
{
    //Base case: word found
    if ($wordIndex === strlen($word)) {
        return true;
    }

    if ($row < 0 || $row >= count($board) || $col < 0 || $col >= count($board[0]) || $board[$row][$col] !== $word[$wordIndex]) {
        return false;
    }

    $temp = $board[$row][$col];
    $board[$row][$col] = ''; // Mark current cell as visited

    // Explore adjacent cells
    $found = wordSearchRecursive($board, $word, $row + 1, $col, $wordIndex + 1) ||
             wordSearchRecursive($board, $word, $row - 1, $col, $wordIndex + 1) ||
             wordSearchRecursive($board, $word, $row, $col + 1, $wordIndex + 1) ||
             wordSearchRecursive($board, $word, $row, $col - 1, $wordIndex + 1);

    // Backtrack: restore the original value
    $board[$row][$col] = $temp;

    return $found;
}

?>

<?php
function wordSearch(array $board, string $word): bool
{
    $rows = count($board);
    $cols = count($board[0]);

    for ($row = 0; $row < $rows; $row++) {
        for ($col = 0; $col < $cols; $col++) {
            // Start DFS from each cell.
            if (wordSearchRecursive($board, $word, $row, $col, 0)) {
                return true;
            }
        }
    }

    return false;
}

function wordSearchRecursive(array $board, string $word, int $row, int $col, int $wordIndex): bool
{
    //Base case: word found
    if ($wordIndex === strlen($word)) {
        return true;
    }

    if ($row < 0 || $row >= count($board) || $col < 0 || $col >= count($board[0]) || $board[$row][$col] !== $word[$wordIndex]) {
        return false;
    }

    $temp = $board[$row][$col];
    $board[$row][$col] = ''; // Mark current cell as visited

    // Explore adjacent cells
    $found = wordSearchRecursive($board, $word, $row + 1, $col, $wordIndex + 1) ||
             wordSearchRecursive($board, $word, $row - 1, $col, $wordIndex + 1) ||
             wordSearchRecursive($board, $word, $row, $col + 1, $wordIndex + 1) ||
             wordSearchRecursive($board, $word, $row, $col - 1, $wordIndex + 1);

    // Backtrack: restore the original value
    $board[$row][$col] = $temp;

    return $found;
}

?>
